data.title: Revere Control Systems wins Groundbreaker Award in Birmingham Business Journal's 2025 Manufacturing Awards for innovation.
data.description: In Hoover, Alabama on October 16th, 2025, Revere Control Systems, a subsidiary of SJE, Inc., was honored with the Groundbreaker Award during the Birmingham Business Journal's 2025 Manufacturing Awards. This prestigious recognition highlights the company's innovative leadership and significant contributions within Alabama's manufacturing sector.

data.title: Salem Media Group issues SALM stock to co-founder Atsinger III instead of cash, removes stock forfeiture provision.
data.description: Salem Media Group recently announced the issuance of SALM stock to Edward G. Atsinger III, the Co-Founder, and Executive Chairman. This issuance was made in place of cash payment. The move signifies a strategic transition for the company. Atsinger has been an integral part of the organization, contributing significantly to its growth and success. The decision to issue stock to him underscores his continued importance to the company. This development also resulted in the removal of the existing stock repurchase agreement. The specific number of SALM stocks issued to Atsinger has not been disclosed publicly.

data.title: National Geographic unveils new special featuring Chris Hemsworth on a personal journey, highlighting dementia - 57M affected worldwide, 10M new cases yearly.
data.description: In global news today, it has been reported that over 57 million individuals are currently facing the challenges of dementia on a worldwide scale, with Alzheimer’s disease emerging as the most prevalent cause. Shockingly, an additional 10 million new cases of dementia surface each year. This alarming statistic prompts us to contemplate what actions we can take to support those impacted by this condition. In light of this, Chris Hemsworth is embarking on an intimate journey in his latest initiative - CHRIS HEMSWORTH: A ROAD TRIP TO REMEMBER, transcending his previous health-centric projects, such as the LIMITLESS series.

data.title: Susan Stamberg, trailblazer at NPR, passes away at 87. First woman to anchor national evening news in 1972, retired after 50 years on air.
data.description: In 1972, she made history as the inaugural woman to lead a national evening news program. Wrapping up an illustrious 50-year career on television, she bid farewell this summer, leaving a lasting impact on the industry.

data.title: Interactive Brokers Group Inc. reported strong Q3 earnings with record net revenues of $537 million, up 23% YoY. Commission revenue increased by 23%, net interest income by 21%, and client equity reached three quarters of a trillion dollars, up 40% YoY. They added 790,000 net new accounts in Q3, exceeding last year's total. The company continues to see growing interest in crypto trading.
data.description: Interactive Brokers Group Inc. reported their third-quarter earnings with record net revenues and pre-tax income. The company's commission revenue increased by 23% compared to last year, and net interest income reached a quarterly record of $967 million. The company added their 4 millionth customer and reached $150 billion in client cash balances, up over 30% from last year. Client equity surpassed three quarters of a trillion dollars, showing a 40% increase from last year. Additionally, the company added 790,000 net new accounts through the third quarter, exceeding the total added last year. Client trading volumes expanded, especially in stocks and options, contributing to a 21% increase in total net revenues. The company saw growth in options and equity volumes, with record numbers in options contracts. The company also experienced increasing activity in cryptocurrency forecast contracts and overnight trading hours, with significant rises in trade volumes. Interactive Brokers continued to introduce enhancements to their platform, including new liquidity providers for options and securities, further enhancing execution quality. The company focuses on global investing opportunities and serving clients' needs with a lower cost structure and a broader product range. The company ranked fourth in the number of hedge funds serviced, illustrating its competitive edge in the prime brokerage industry. The company looks forward to further developments in the upcoming years.

data.title: Dubai partners with DePIN peaq network to regulate machine economy, integrating onchain robotics, AI, and tokenized assets.
data.description: Dubai has taken a significant step towards regulating the "machine economy" through a new partnership. This initiative integrates on-chain robotics, AI technology, and tokenized real-world assets in the region. This move underscores Dubai's commitment to embracing innovative technologies and advancing towards a digitally-driven future.

data.title: Entegris (NASDAQ: ENTG) will release Q3 2025 results on Oct 30, 2025. Teleconference at 8:00am ET. Dial +1 800-579-2543 for details.
data.description: Entegris, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENTG) based in Billerica, Massachusetts, will announce its financial results for the third quarter of 2025 on Thursday, October 30, 2025, before the market opens. A teleconference with management will take place at 8:00am ET on the same day. Those interested can join by dialing either +1 800-579-2543 or +1 785-424-1789 using the Conference ID: ENTGQ325. Participants are advised to dial in 5 to 10 minutes prior to the call. Additionally, a live webcast and replay of the call will be available.

data.title: NerdWallet to hold conference call to discuss 2025 Q3 financial results.
data.description: San Francisco-based financial website NerdWallet has scheduled a conference call to discuss its financial results for the third quarter of 2025. The company will be reviewing important numbers such as revenue, profits, and any other relevant financial data from that time period during the call. This announcement was made through a press release on Business Wire.

data.title: Stock market falls as regional bank fears rise. Interactive Brokers and Oracle slipped late. Dow Jones futures affected.
data.description: On Thursday, the stock market experienced a downturn due to concerns surrounding regional banks, leading to a spike in fear among investors. This resulted in declines for Interactive Brokers and Oracle later in the day. Dow Jones Futures were impacted as well, with the stock market falling amidst these bank fears, causing Oracle to slip in late trading. Investors are advised to reassess their strategies and consider the current market conditions.

data.title: Gold and silver prices rise as stock index falls; Bank stocks sell off. NuScale sees big swings. via @IBDinvestors
data.description: Today in the stock market, NuScale saw an increase in activity, experiencing significant fluctuations that could impact traders with substantial movements in both upward and downward directions. Additionally, gold and silver prices rose as overall index losses continued to rise, leading to a sell-off of bank stocks. Live coverage of these developments is available on Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Disc Medicine obtained FDA's CNPV for bitopertin in EPP, awaiting accelerated approval NDA, aiding patients aged 12+.
data.description: On October 16, 2025, Disc Medicine, Inc. (NASDAQ:IRON), a biopharmaceutical company in Watertown, Mass., revealed it had obtained a Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher (CNPV) from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for bitopertin, a drug aimed at treating erythropoietic protoporphyria (EPP) and X-linked protoporphyria (XLP). This voucher was granted after the company submitted a New Drug Application (NDA) for bitopertin to the FDA, specifically targeting patients aged 12 and above with EPP. The drug had been fast-tracked for approval by the FDA in September 2025. Bitopertin had previously been granted Orphan Drug Designation and Rare Pediatric Disease Designation by the FDA.
